Statement: Every whole number has a predecessor.
Every whole number has a successor.
0 has a predecessor in whole numbers.
Addition of whole numbers is closed.
Subtraction of whole numbers is closed.
Multiplication of whole numbers is closed.
Division of whole numbers is closed.
0 is the additive identity for whole numbers.
1 is the multiplicative identity for whole numbers.
Any whole number multiplied by 1 gives the same number.
The successor of the largest 3-digit whole number is 1001.
a + b = b + a for all whole numbers a and b.
(a + b) + c = a + (b + c) for all whole numbers.
a × (b + c) = a × b + a × c.
a × b = b × a for all whole numbers.
0 is both a whole number and a natural number.
The difference of two whole numbers is always a whole number.
The sum of two whole numbers is always a whole number.
The product of two whole numbers is always a whole number.
The smallest whole number greater than 999 is 1000.
Every whole number except 0 has a predecessor.
The successor of any whole number is unique.
The predecessor of any whole number is unique.
